task: Implemented macOS voice notification system for askr init to enable users to opt-in to spoken state updates via native `say` command, integrated with existing Discord notification hooks to announce task completion and session state transitions.
discussion_summary: Session focused on voice notification preference schema design and initial implementation. Determined that voice_notifications is a machine-level user trait (stored in global ~/.config/askr/config.json) rather than per-project, mirroring the pattern of developer name. Added load_voice_enabled() and save_voice_enabled() helper functions to askr/state/config.py following existing config.py conventions. Architecture identified: voice notifications hook into existing notification infrastructure (askr/hooks/stop.py, notification.py) as an additional sink alongside Discord, requiring minimal mechanical changes to wire opt-in preference through init flow and state management.

accomplishments.what: LinkedIn location combobox field filling fixed with city name extraction and fallback retry pattern
Identified root cause of LinkedIn location field failures: full location strings do not trigger city autocomplete dropdown
Implemented two-part fix: prompt instructs extraction of city name from full location string, with retry on failure
Killed orphaned uvicorn process blocking backend logs
Conducted comprehensive security audit of apply agent code generation paths with four hardening fixes against prompt injection attacks
Fixed resume PDF portfolio URL lookup from qa_bank.portfolio_url to application_prefill.answers.portfolio_url
Updated PDF generator to render 'Portfolio' as link label instead of domain URL
Diagnosed Ramp application failure as Ashby spam_warning state (browser fingerprinting-based anti-bot detection)
Implemented spam_warning recovery with 'Learn more' probe to locate submit/submit-again button
Extended spam_warning handling to distinguish overlay banner (resubmit after scroll) vs form replacement (hard refresh required)
Refactored spam recovery strategy to defer spam-flagged jobs to end of session instead of inline retry
Investigated queue drain architecture and browser_stream replay buffer lifecycle
Scoped voice notification feature: confirmed macOS-only deployment enables native `say` TTS without cross-platform abstraction
Identified notification hook integration points (askr/hooks/stop.py, notification.py) for wiring voice updates as additional sink alongside Discord
Designed voice notification preference schema: machine-level boolean flag stored in global ~/.config/askr/config.json (not per-project)
Implemented load_voice_enabled() and save_voice_enabled() helper functions in askr/state/config.py following existing config pattern

in_progress.what: Architectural design for stateful retry mechanism that captures failure context (screenshots, error reasoning) to enable learning-based job resubmission instead of blind retry
Voice notification system implementation: wire voice_enabled preference through askr init flow, integrate with existing notification hooks to announce task completion and session state transitions

next_actions.action: Implement voice preference prompt in askr init flow (mirror Discord webhook prompt pattern) to capture user opt-in during initialization
next_actions.why: Required before voice notifications can be triggered; determines UX for first-time setup and preference changes

next_actions.action: Create askr/hooks/voice.py notification sink that wraps macOS `say` command with error handling and optional volume/rate parameters
next_actions.why: Implements the actual TTS invocation; should follow notification.py Discord sink pattern for consistency

next_actions.action: Wire voice_enabled preference check into askr/hooks/stop.py and notification.py to conditionally invoke voice sink alongside Discord webhook
next_actions.why: Connects preference to actual notification dispatch; minimal mechanical change to existing hook infrastructure

next_actions.action: Add voice notification test coverage for load_voice_enabled/save_voice_enabled in tests/test_config.py
next_actions.why: Ensures preference persistence and retrieval work correctly; follows existing test patterns

decisions.decision: Voice notification preference stored as machine-level boolean in global ~/.config/askr/config.json, not per-project
decisions.reason: Voice is a user/machine trait (do I want this Mac to talk), not a per-project secret like discord_webhook; mirrors developer name pattern in config.py

decisions.decision: Voice notifications implemented as additional sink in existing notification infrastructure, not separate code path
decisions.reason: Minimizes mechanical changes; reuses Discord webhook pattern (notification.py, hooks/stop.py) for consistency and maintainability

decisions.decision: macOS-only deployment using native `say` command without cross-platform abstraction
decisions.reason: askr is macOS-only; native TTS avoids external dependencies and licensing complexity

relational_files.why: Notification dispatch point where voice sink will be wired alongside Discord webhook
Existing notification sink pattern that voice implementation will mirror
Init flow where voice preference prompt will be added (mirror Discord webhook prompt)
